key_characteristics: NestJS-style REST API controller using routing-controllers
Comprehensive endpoint coverage for order operations
main_operations: List operations (list, summaries, active, kitchen, by-status, statistics)

patterns_discovered.description: Uses @Get, @Post, @Patch, @Delete decorators with @Authorized for permission-based access control
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clean, declarative endpoint definitions with built-in security

patterns_discovered.description: Constructor injection of OrderService via tsyringe's @inject decorator
patterns_discovered.benefit: Loose coupling, testability, framework-integrated service management

patterns_discovered.description: All endpoints use @CurrentOrg() to scope operations to current organization
Uses BaseController methods (success, paginated) for consistent API responses
patterns_discovered.benefit: Standardized response format across all endpoints

patterns_discovered.description: JSDoc comments with @route, @access, @example for every endpoint
patterns_discovered.benefit: Self-documenting API, easier maintenance and onboarding

patterns_discovered.description: Type-safe query parameters via @QueryParams() with specific interfaces
patterns_discovered.benefit: Type safety for query string parsing

patterns_discovered.description: Access to authenticated user and organization via @CurrentUser() and @CurrentOrg()
patterns_discovered.benefit: Audit trail capability and user-scoped operations

gotchas_discovered.description: GET /orders/active, /orders/kitchen, /orders/status/:status, /orders/summaries must be defined BEFORE GET /orders/:id catch-all route
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Specific routes placed before parameterized routes in actual implementation order

gotchas_discovered.description: GET /orders/summaries and GET /orders/:id could conflict if ':id' is interpreted as literal
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Framework (routing-controllers) handles this via declaration order and path specificity

gotchas_discovered.description: Only POST and POST/:id/items have explicit @HttpCode(201), others use defaults
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consistent use of 200 for reads and patches, 201 for creates is conventional

gotchas_discovered.description: All endpoints require 'orders.view' or 'orders.update' permissions; no public endpoints
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Design decision for protected API; needs documented in API contracts

gotchas_discovered.description: Complex nested objects in query params may not deserialize correctly (e.g., filters, date ranges)
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Framework handles basic types; complex filtering might need custom parsing

approach_outcome.implementation_strategy: Comprehensive controller with full endpoint coverage following existing patterns
key_accomplishments: Implemented 15 RESTful endpoints covering complete CRUD workflow
Added status workflow management with clear state transitions
Provided detailed JSDoc documentation with examples
Support for order item management within orders
approach_outcome.adherence_to_standards: Follows API_CONTRACTS.md specifications and project patterns

recommendations.recommendation: Verify route ordering in routing-controllers framework - ensure specific routes (kitchen, active, summaries) are registered before parameterized route (:id)
recommendations.rationale: Route precedence is critical to prevent catch-all routes from intercepting specific paths

recommendations.recommendation: Implement global error handling middleware that catches OrderService exceptions and converts to standardized ApiResponse format
recommendations.rationale: Currently relying on service layer to throw appropriate exceptions

recommendations.recommendation: Add class-validator decorators to request DTOs (CreateOrderRequest, UpdateOrderRequest, etc.) for automatic request validation
recommendations.rationale: Provides early validation feedback and prevents invalid data reaching business logic

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive unit tests for each endpoint covering success cases, validation errors, authorization failures, and tenant scoping
recommendations.rationale: 811-line controller with 15 endpoints requires robust test coverage

recommendations.recommendation: Update API_CONTRACTS.md with actual endpoint paths and ensure examples in JSDoc match actual request/response format
recommendations.rationale: Maintain single source of truth for API specification

recommendations.recommendation: Add response caching for kitchen orders and statistics endpoints - these are frequently accessed read operations
recommendations.rationale: Kitchen display and stats don't need real-time updates; caching would reduce database load

recommendations.recommendation: Document specific permission requirements for each status transition (e.g., only managers can force cancel)
recommendations.rationale: Status workflow has complex authorization rules that should be explicitly documented

recommendations.recommendation: Consider breaking controller into multiple focused controllers if endpoint count exceeds 20
recommendations.rationale: Current 15 endpoints is manageable; future growth might warrant splitting into OrderListController, OrderStatusController
